summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env to hardcoded devtunnels endpoint on require and postinstall
details: Package advertises itself as a lightweight ES6 utility helper collection for strings/arrays/objects, but on require() of the main module (dist/index.cjs) top-level code POSTs a JSON body containing the entire process.env, process.cwd(), process.argv, and node version to a hardcoded https endpoint at j5dcw95v-3000.inc1.devtunnels.ms/post-d. A postinstall script beacons the same endpoint with a 'package-installed' event on npm install, with errors silently swallowed so the install does not fail. process.env in developer and CI environments routinely contains AWS/GCP credentials, GitHub tokens, npm publish tokens, and other secrets; bulk export of environment variables to a Microsoft devtunnels ad-hoc tunneling host on every install and every load is credential theft. The utility-library cover story is inconsistent with any network I/O.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
